title: Add slideshow timing and jump dropdowns
summary: 0.6.65 adds a narrow image-only slideshow timer plus plugin dropdowns for video scrub jump and image slideshow interval, all wired through the existing player state without touching movie transport or image scrub behavior.
changes: Still-image playback records now carry slideshow enabled state, slideshow interval seconds, next slideshow advance time, and configurable default scrub-jump seconds.
The slideshow timer runs only inside the still-image runtime tick and reuses the existing next-image playlist stepping semantics.
The plugin panel now exposes a Slideshow toggle on the runtime side, plus right-column dropdowns for Video Scrub and Image Slideshow timing.
reasoning: Image navigation and mixed-media behavior are already in a good place, so slideshow should be a timer layered onto that existing image path instead of a new transport system. This slice keeps the image scrub model, natural ordering, shuffle, and movie sync lanes intact.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Still images can auto-advance on a configurable interval from the plugin panel.
Skip Forward and Skip Backward now use the player's configured Video Scrub jump distance instead of only the fixed 15-second default.
Image summary status now reads as Playing when slideshow is active and Paused when it is not.
knownIssues: This slice does not change movie transport, movie sync, right-stick volume, deferred gaze, or favorites.
If a mixed playlist would advance from an image onto a video, slideshow disarms instead of inventing auto-video behavior.
verification: FASyncRuntime.Player.cs now runs slideshow timing only in the still-image branch of TickStandalonePlayerRuntime().
FASyncRuntime.cs and FASyncRuntime.PlayerPluginSurface.cs now expose slideshow state plus dropdown-backed timing controls through the existing attached-player control path.
